ogTitle: Landervale vs Lane Cove North: Climate Safety
ogDescription: Data-driven comparison of climate risk in Landervale and Lane Cove North.
heading: Landervale vs Lane Cove North — Which is Safer?
intro: Compare climate risk between Landervale, New South Wales and Lane Cove North, New South Wales. Our analysis uses data from BOM, CSIRO, and state hazard authorities to score each suburb across 6 climate hazards.
verdict: Landervale has a lower overall climate risk score (29/100) compared to Lane Cove North (38/100). We recommend getting an address-level report for properties in both suburbs before making a purchase decision.

faq.question: Is Landervale or Lane Cove North safer from flooding?
faq.answer: Landervale has a flood risk score of 24/100, while Lane Cove North scores 22/100. A lower score indicates lower flood risk. Get an address-level flood risk report for a specific property.

faq.question: Which suburb has lower bushfire risk?
faq.answer: Landervale scores 19/100 for bushfire risk. Lane Cove North scores 50/100. Fire risk depends on proximity to vegetation and local fire history.

faq.question: Should I buy in Landervale or Lane Cove North?
faq.answer: Both suburbs have climate considerations. Landervale has a lower overall climate risk score. We recommend getting a full ClimateNest report for any specific property you're considering, which includes 8 hazard scores, insurance projections, and actionable recommendations.

valueImpact.description: Properties in this area may face a 1.2% climate value drag over time due to rising insurance costs and climate adaptation expenses.
